chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, BENCH AT SUKKUR Cr. Bail Applicatio. No. D – 14 of 2024 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF HON’BLE JUDGE Fresh case 1. For orders on MA No.141/2024 (U/A) 2. For orders on office objection at flag ‘A’ 3. For orders on MA No.142/2024 (Ex.A) 4. For hearing of bail application 15.02.2024 Mr. Munir Ahmed Kalhoro, Advocate for the Applicant >>>>>..<<<<< 1. Urgency granted. 2&3. Deferred. 4. The facts in brief necessary for disposal of instant bail application u/s 498 Cr.P.C are that the applicant on having been involved in Reference No.07/2019 by NAB authorities filed a petition No.D-665/2019, whereby he initially was admitted to interim pre-arrest bail by this Court, during pendency whereof the NAB law was amended and accused involved therein were provided a chance to seek bail in accordance with law; consequently, petition filed by the petitioner was converted into an application for protective bail and he was given 10 days’ time to surrender before the NAB Court concerned. The applicant approached the NAB Court concerned for the purpose and was admitted to interim pre-arrest bail and then preferred to go in absconsion for about two years. Now by way of instant bail application, has again sought for protective bail to surrender before NAB Court concerned to seek pre-arrest bail. Law favours equity and fair play. No justification is advanced by the applicant to admit him to protective bail again, ignoring his long absconsion and earlier orders of this Court. Consequent upon above, the instant bail application for grant of protective bail to the applicant fails and is dismissed in limine.

No justification is advanced by the applicant to admit him to protective bail again, ignoring his long absconsion and earlier orders of this Court. Consequent upon above, the instant bail application for grant of protective bail to the applicant fails and is dismissed in limine. Judge Judge ARBROHI
